notes: Merge output was structurally clean: input 463/639 == output 463/639, no nodes with missing ids, no duplicate ids, no dangling edges, no required-field gaps.
All node types valid (function 259, file 116, class 86, document 1, config 1) and all complexity values valid (simple 143, moderate 240, complex 80). Nothing to remap.
Empty C# importMap is expected (namespace using-directives, not file-path imports); absence of 'imports' edges is not an error. Existing cross-file links use depends_on/calls/related.

Found 4 fully orphaned files (Models/ThemePalette.cs, Models/ImageStyle.cs, Models/ImagePlanItem.cs, Services/Rx.cs) with no edges. These are real cross-batch gaps: the model/helper landed in a different batch than its consumers, so no batch emitted the file->file depends_on edge.

Added 14 code-verified depends_on edges (direction forward, weight 0.7) wiring service-layer/MainWindow consumers to these definitions, confirmed by grep of actual type usage: ThemePalette<-{ThemeRandomizer,AiCssGenerator,AiImageGenerator,ImagePromptCatalog,PaletteStore}; ImageStyle<-{ImageStyleCatalog,ImageRenderCatalog,GenerationOptions}; ImagePlanItem<-{ImagePromptCatalog,ImagePlanBuilder,AiImageGenerator,GeneratedImageWriter}; Rx<-{MainWindow.Site,ContentStore}.

Remaining fully isolated nodes (.understandignore, AppVersion.props, AssemblyInfo.cs, GlobalUsings.cs, Themes/Generic.xaml) are legitimately standalone metadata/config/global-resource files and were intentionally left unconnected.

No speculative edges added; every new edge is backed by a verified type reference in source. depends_on convention in this graph is file-level (133/135 pre-existing depends_on edges were file->file), so file-level edges are consistent.
